text.value: Laboratory Report
Laboratory studies

tr.td: Ketones [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

Hemoglobin [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

Epithelial cells [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field

Mucus [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopy
Bacteria [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field
Bacteria identified in Urine by Culture

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Urinalysis shows significant leukocyturia, bacteriuria, positive nitrites, and hematuria, with E. coli >100,000 CFU/mL, indicating a urinary tract infection. The presence of glucose in urine is abnormal and may warrant further evaluation for hyperglycemia or diabetes. Low specific gravity suggests dilute urine. No casts detected. Recommend clinical correlation and follow-up for infection and possible metabolic assessment.

text.value: Carmelo Antonini

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Ospedaliera Sant'Antonio Abate - Gallarate
